title: Group Debug Session
subtitle: Find Bugs, Fix Problems, Learn Together!
overview: This session introduces students to debugging concepts through group activities and real-world coding examples. Students learn how to identify errors, test circuits, solve problems collaboratively, and improve their logical thinking skills.

images._note: Add photos from the actual debug session — students checking circuits, debugging code, testing LEDs, Arduino setups, teamwork moments.

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Students working together on debugging activity
gallery.caption: Students solving coding bugs together
Finding syntax errors in Arduino code

keyTopics: What is Debugging? — Finding and fixing mistakes in code or circuits
Syntax Error — Mistakes in writing code
Logic Error — Code runs but output is wrong
Connection Error — Wrong wiring in the circuit
Testing Step-by-Step — Check one part at a time
Serial Monitor — Used to find program errors
Team Debugging — Discuss and solve problems together
Trial and Error — Learn by testing repeatedly

activity.title: Find the Bug Challenge
steps: Teacher provides a circuit with intentional mistakes
Students observe the LED not blinking properly
Read Arduino code and identify syntax errors
Discuss how the issue was solved as a team

exampleDebugCase.title: LED Not Blinking Example
exampleDebugCase.problem: The LED does not blink even after uploading the program.
possibleErrors: Wrong Arduino pin number in code
solutionSteps: Check LED polarity — long leg should connect to positive
Verify pin number in code matches circuit
Fix code syntax and upload again
exampleDebugCase.result: LED starts blinking successfully after debugging.

quiz.explanation: Debugging means finding and fixing mistakes in code or circuits.

quiz.q: Which tool helps check Arduino output messages?
quiz.explanation: Serial Monitor helps programmers see messages and debug Arduino programs.

quiz.q: If the LED is connected in reverse, what may happen?
quiz.explanation: LEDs work only in one direction. Reverse connection stops it from glowing.
Syntax errors occur when coding rules or spelling are incorrect.

quiz.q: What is the best way to debug a circuit?
quiz.explanation: Good debugging means checking each part carefully one-by-one.

quiz.q: Why is teamwork important in debugging?
options: More people can find mistakes faster
quiz.explanation: Group discussion helps identify errors and solutions more quickly.

options: Program runs but gives wrong result
quiz.explanation: Logic errors happen when code runs but output is incorrect.

quiz.q: Which component protects the LED from too much current?
quiz.explanation: A resistor limits current and protects the LED.

quiz.q: What should students do first when debugging?
quiz.explanation: Start by checking connections and components carefully.
After fixing errors properly, the project runs successfully.
